questions.question: A random variable X has a probability mass function given by f(x) = 1/2 for x = -1, 0, 1 and f(x) = 0 otherwise. Find the value of P(X = 0).
For a continuous random variable with a probability density function f(x) = 2x for 0 ≤ x ≤ 1, find the value of P(0 < X < 1/2).
questions.hint: Use the definition of the probability density function

questions.question: A discrete random variable X has the following probability mass function: P(X = 1) = 0.4, P(X = 2) = 0.3, P(X = 3) = 0.3. Find the value of P(X ≤ 2).
questions.hint: Use the fact that the sum of probabilities of all possible values of X is 1
